page: Amazon Web Services r8a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Geekbench metrics. Memory performance is highly competitive, with read-only small block memory bandwidth and large block write bandwidth reaching top-tier status, while large block read bandwidth is rated as strong. The processor's instruction set supports AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, contributing to top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and software compilation.

In terms of resource tradeoffs, the r8a.12xlarge does not include local NVMe SSD storage or dedicated GPU hardware, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. This configuration offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads by maximizing DDR5 memory capacity and memory bandwidth per vCPU without the added expense of specialized accelerator hardware.

This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, including large-scale relational databases, in-memory data stores like Redis, software compilation pipelines, and medium-scale language model inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It is configured with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, delivering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides 22 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance in multi-core processing, database operations, and memory bandwidth, alongside strong performance in large-block memory reads. A key tradeoff is the absence of local storage and GPU accelerators, which optimizes the instance's cost efficiency specifically for memory-bound workloads. The r8a.12xlarge is highly suited for in-memory databases, Redis deployments, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 22 Gbps network speed, delivering top-tier database and multi-core CPU benchmark performance.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 RAM for demanding database and memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
384 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis, and software compilation
Tradeoffs include lack of local storage and GPU accelerators
